book_claims.claim: Trout recommended as a high omega-3, omega-6, and vitamin B12 fish for the Longevity Diet
book_claims.mechanism: Oily freshwater fish with high EPA/DHA content and low mercury levels

book_claims.claim: Trout is a source of vitamin B12 (5 mcg per 75 g cooked, 83% DV), calcium (123 mg per fillet wild cooked, 12% DV), vitamin D (539 IU per fillet rainbow farmed cooked, 134.75% DV), and omega-3 (0.65–0.87 g EPA/DHA per 75 g cooked)
book_claims.mechanism: Multi-nutrient fish rich in vitamin B12, D, calcium, and omega-3 EPA/DHA

supplementary_research.finding: Rainbow trout is identified as a selenium-rich freshwater fish (PMID 20699226); selenium from trout is highly bioavailable and supports antioxidant defense via selenoproteins
Trout EPA/DHA content (PMID 22129636) is comparable to salmon and sardines; regular consumption associates with reduced cardiovascular mortality in cohort studies
Trout provides one of the highest natural vitamin D concentrations among freshwater fish; combined with its omega-3 and B12 content it is among the most nutrient-dense longevity foods

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Exceptionally high natural vitamin D3; fat in fish significantly aids absorption
Organic selenocysteine; supports glutathione peroxidase and antioxidant function
Supports cardiovascular health and blood pressure regulation

synergies.description: Classic French pairing; almonds' vitamin E complements trout's omega-3s for combined anti-inflammatory effect
Rotating between trout and salmon diversifies omega-3 sources and micronutrient profiles while maintaining low mercury intake
Vitamin C aids absorption of trout's non-heme iron; brightens flavor
